description: Deep merge two objects with recursive object merging and array concatenation
prompt: Write a JavaScript ES module that exports a function `deepMerge(target, source)` which deeply merges `source` into `target` and returns a new object (do not mutate the inputs). The merge rules are: for plain objects, recurse and merge properties; for arrays, concatenate them (target array + source array); for all other types, source value overwrites target. Treat `null` as a primitive (not an object to recurse into). Export as named export.

tests.name: merges flat objects
tests.code: const result = mod.deepMerge({a: 1, b: 2}, {b: 3, c: 4}); assert(result.a === 1, 'Expected a=1'); assert(result.b === 3, 'Expected b=3 (overwritten)'); assert(result.c === 4, 'Expected c=4');

tests.name: recursively merges nested objects
tests.code: const result = mod.deepMerge({a: {x: 1, y: 2}}, {a: {y: 3, z: 4}}); assert(result.a.x === 1, 'Expected a.x=1'); assert(result.a.y === 3, 'Expected a.y=3'); assert(result.a.z === 4, 'Expected a.z=4');

tests.name: concatenates arrays
tests.code: const result = mod.deepMerge({tags: ['a', 'b']}, {tags: ['c']}); assert(JSON.stringify(result.tags) === JSON.stringify(['a', 'b', 'c']), 'Expected [a,b,c] but got ' + JSON.stringify(result.tags));

tests.name: handles null values as primitives
tests.code: const result = mod.deepMerge({a: {x: 1}}, {a: null}); assert(result.a === null, 'Expected null to overwrite object'); const result2 = mod.deepMerge({a: null}, {a: {x: 1}}); assert(result2.a.x === 1, 'Expected object to overwrite null');

tests.name: does not mutate original objects
tests.code: const target = {a: {x: 1}}; const source = {a: {y: 2}}; const result = mod.deepMerge(target, source); assert(target.a.y === undefined, 'Target should not be mutated'); assert(source.a.x === undefined, 'Source should not be mutated'); assert(result.a.x === 1 && result.a.y === 2, 'Result should have merged values');
